SEARCH

Class InfixPostfix

java.lang.Object
InfixPostfix
public class InfixPostfix extends Object
Provides static methods for evaluating and converting arithmetic expressions in postfix (RPN) and infix notation. All tokens in an expression must be separated by single spaces. Supported operators are: + - * / ^ ( ) Operands must be integers. The ^ operator is right-associative.
Version:
4/7/26
Author:
Ali Kablanbek

    static int
    evaluateInfix(String expression)
    Evaluates a full infix expression by converting it to postfix first then evaluating the result.
    static int

    Evaluates a postfix expression and returns the integer result.
    static String
    infixToPostfix(String expression)
    Converts a full infix expression to postfix notation using the Shunting-Yard algorithm.

    Reads infix expressions line by line from an input file, evaluates each one with evaluateInfix() and writes the results to an output file.

  • Constructor Details

    • InfixPostfix

      public InfixPostfix()

  • Method Details

    • evaluatePostfix

      public static int evaluatePostfix(String expression) throws ExpressionFormatException
      Evaluates a postfix expression and returns the integer result. Tokens must be separated by spaces. Example: "5 1 2 + 4 * + 3 -" evaluates to 14.
      Parameters:
      expression - space-separated postfix expression
      Returns:
      integer result of the expression
      Throws:
      ExpressionFormatException - if the expression contains invalid tokens, has the wrong number of operands or causes division by zero

    • simpleInfixToPostfix

      public static String simpleInfixToPostfix(String expression) throws ExpressionFormatException
      Converts a simple infix expression to postfix notation. Parentheses and the ^ operator are not allowed. Tokens must be separated by spaces. Example: "3 + 4 * 5" converts to "3 4 5 * +".
      Parameters:
      expression - space-separated simple infix expression
      Returns:
      equivalent postfix expression
      Throws:
      ExpressionFormatException - if the expression is invalid or contains parentheses or exponentiation

    • infixToPostfix

      public static String infixToPostfix(String expression) throws ExpressionFormatException
      Converts a full infix expression to postfix notation using the Shunting-Yard algorithm. Handles + - * / ^ and parentheses. The ^ operator is right-associative. Tokens must be separated by spaces. Example: "( 3 + 4 ) * 5" converts to "3 4 + 5 *".
      Parameters:
      expression - space-separated infix expression
      Returns:
      equivalent postfix expression
      Throws:
      ExpressionFormatException - if the expression has mismatched parentheses, unknown tokens or incorrect operand placement

    • evaluateInfix

      public static int evaluateInfix(String expression) throws ExpressionFormatException
      Evaluates a full infix expression by converting it to postfix first then evaluating the result. Tokens must be separated by spaces.
      Parameters:
      expression - space-separated infix expression
      Returns:
      integer result of the expression
      Throws:
      ExpressionFormatException - if conversion or evaluation fails

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Reads infix expressions line by line from an input file, evaluates each one with evaluateInfix() and writes the results to an output file. Each successful output line takes the form: expression = result If a line causes an ExpressionFormatException the output line is: expression -- ERROR: message Usage: java InfixPostfix inputFile outputFile
      Parameters:
      args - args[0] is the input file path and args[1] is the output file path
